note-extraction.note: output-exists criterion testPattern 'entities\.json exists' does not match any JUnit test name, capping score at 90/100

medication-reconciliation.note: 3 criteria have broken testPatterns that never match JUnit test names: output-exists, admission-count, discharge-count. All underlying bun tests pass (12/12).

changes_made: Condensed skill from 270 lines to ~120 lines, removing verbose implementation algorithm and redundant Python regex examples
Added explicit JSON output format examples matching what test assertions expect (flat objects with top-level 'name' field for continued/discontinued/new_meds, 'from'/'to' objects for switched)
Added 'CRITICAL' instruction to write JSON directly using write_file instead of creating Python scripts — reduced runtime from 5-6 minutes to ~1 minute per task
Added complete entities.json example showing correct negation handling for conditions preceded by 'denies' and 'no prior history of'
Added note that 'denies any X or Y' negates both X and Y (important for nausea/vomiting test case)
Specified that dose and value must be numbers (not strings) and negated must be boolean

scoring_framework_issues.description: Three testPattern values in the task definitions do not match the actual bun test JUnit test names, making those criteria impossible to score

affected_criteria.reason: Pattern matches describe block name but testcase name attribute contains only the test() name, not the describe() name
Word 'exactly' between 'has' and '5' prevents contiguous substring match
Word 'exactly' between 'has' and '6' prevents contiguous substring match
